headline: China cancels high-level EU meetings as Xi visits North Korea, signaling a sharp geopolitical pivot
headline_zh: 习近平访朝之际取消与欧盟高级别会议，外交转向信号强烈
narrative: The narrative splits in two today. On one side, Xi Jinping's state visit to North Korea, his first in years, reaffirms the China-DPRK axis and pushes back against U.S.-led pressure. On the other, Beijing has abruptly cancelled high-level meetings with the EU, according to the Financial Times, a serious escalation of trade and diplomatic tensions. The message is clear: Beijing prioritizes its near-abroad and adversarial posture over Western engagement. Domestically, the state is also tightening: auto firms were warned against 'irrational competition', and new anti-dumping duties on Korean and Thai chemicals signal that industrial policy and trade defense are the new normal. Meanwhile, AI short-drama startup AniShort raised nearly 100 million yuan, a bright spot in the tech funding winter.
narrative_zh: 今日叙事呈现两条主线：习近平对朝鲜进行国事访问，巩固中朝关系，反制美国压力；同时，据《金融时报》报道，中方突然取消与欧盟的高级别会议，贸易外交紧张升级。信号明确：北京优先经营周边并维持对抗姿态。国内层面，两部委约谈车企警告非理性竞争，对韩泰马共聚聚甲醛加征反倾销税，显示产业政策和贸易防御成为常态。另一方面，AI短剧工具AniShort获近亿元融资，成资本寒冬中的亮点。
